scope: r7 stack speedups (FP8 + CUDA graphs) — stacks ON TOP of 7 r6 base patches

stack_context.r5: alm14b r5 currently training — DO NOT DISRUPT
stack_context.r7_target: FP8 + cudagraphs on top of r6
stack_context.r7_policy: Both behaviors default OFF; opt-in via CLI flags so r6 run is bit-identical if flags absent

fp8_uplift_citations: NVIDIA H100 FP8 blog: https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-hopper-architecture-in-depth/ — quotes up to 2x bf16 throughput on GPT-class at 70B
NeurIPS 2022 TE paper (Micikevicius et al., arxiv 2209.05433) — Table 1 reports 1.5-1.8x wall-clock on GPT-3 13B pretrain
Realistic for 14B LoRA: base forward is the hotspot, LoRA adapters are bf16; conservative floor 1.4x on compute → +40% MFU

fp8_accuracy_risk_detail.lora_risk: < 0.05 CE — base is FROZEN, only LoRA (bf16) receives gradients; FP8 amax scaling only affects forward activations on base weights
fp8_accuracy_risk_detail.recommendation: acceptable for r7 fine-tune; validate with 500-step smoke first

hf_qwen_compat.reason: transformer_engine targets NeMo, not HuggingFace. te.Linear / te.LayerNorm / te.TransformerLayer must REPLACE nn.Linear / nn.LayerNorm. Qwen2 modeling_qwen2.py uses nn.Linear directly.
hf_qwen_compat.integration_path_a: monkey-patch nn.Linear -> te.Linear BEFORE AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(). This is what accelerate>=0.30 FP8RecipeKwargs does internally.
hf_qwen_compat.integration_path_b: use accelerate.Accelerator(mixed_precision='fp8') wrapper — requires refactoring to accelerate launcher, heavier change

fp8_lora_compat.pattern: base weights load as te.Linear (fp8 forward); LoRA adapters added after get_peft_model() remain nn.Linear (bf16); hybrid is recommended in TE docs for fine-tune workflows
fp8_lora_compat.gradient_flow: backward goes through te's custom autograd function for base (fp8 amax delayed scaling) and standard autograd for LoRA

fp8_fsdp_compat.note: amax histories are per-rank; TE all-reduces them internally

fp8_torch_compile_compat.issue: TE custom autograd functions introduce graph breaks; inductor cudagraphs hangs on TE ops at torch 2.4 / TE 1.11
fp8_torch_compile_compat.mitigation: when fp8_active, patch forces torch.compile mode='default' (disables cudagraphs AND max-autotune) — safer path

fp8_ga_compat.quantization_policy: PASS — FP8 is a hardware-native dtype (H100 tensor core format), NOT weight quantization. The no-quantization rule scopes to bitsandbytes/gptq/awq/4bit/8bit weight-only quant methods.
fp8_ga_compat.one_shot_best: PASS — patch is applied to a NEW r7 run from step 0

fp8.install_requirement: transformer_engine==1.11 (do NOT install on current r5/r6 pod per explicit policy; install only on fresh r7 pod)

cudagraphs_uplift_citations: torch docs: https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/generated/torch.compile.html — mode='reduce-overhead' is the only mode that activates inductor CUDA graph capture
NVIDIA CUDA Graphs blog: https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/cuda-graphs/ — 20-40% for sub-ms kernels; 14B bf16 step is ~3s so launch overhead is smaller fraction → ~10%
cudagraphs.activation_mechanism: torch.compile(model, mode='reduce-overhead') — changes compile mode from 'max-autotune' to 'reduce-overhead'; these are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE

static_shapes_requirement.evidence: batch=1 fixed, seq=1024 fixed, no sequence packing, LoRA rank fixed → all tensor shapes static across steps
static_shapes_requirement.breaks_if: variable-length seq packing or dynamic batch → graph storm (negative uplift)

cudagraphs.fa2_compat_note: flash-attn>=2.6 kernels are graph-capturable on H100; no issues reported
cudagraphs.fused_adamw_compat_note: optimizer.step() is outside the inductor-captured region
cudagraphs.fsdp_compat_note: use_orig_params=True is required; inductor treats FSDP collectives as graph breaks, captured region is per transformer-block forward+backward

act_ckpt_compat.reason: activation checkpoint recompute introduces dynamic tensor reuse that breaks graph capture
act_ckpt_compat.resolution: r6 base patch already disables activation checkpointing — no conflict

fp8_compat.reason: TE custom autograd has graph breaks; stacking fp8 + cudagraphs causes inductor hangs
fp8_compat.resolution: fp8 patch forces compile mode='default' when fp8_active, overriding --cudagraphs

r6_base_plus_cudagraphs.notes: Best recommended r7 config: r6 + --cudagraphs (keep bf16, gain 10% from graph capture on top of existing r6 50%)

r6_base_plus_fp8.notes: Higher ceiling but requires TE install, smoke validation, and a fresh pod. Accuracy delta is low for LoRA but non-zero.
caveats: torch.compile falls back to mode='default' (loses max-autotune gain ~6%)
net FP8 gain ≈ +40% - 6% ≈ +34% over r6 → +84% vs r5

r6_base_plus_fp8_plus_cudagraphs.reason: fp8 + inductor cudagraphs is known-unstable on torch 2.4 / TE 1.11. Patch explicitly forbids this combination.
r6_base_plus_fp8_plus_cudagraphs.fallback: when both flags set, fp8 wins and cudagraphs is silently disabled
r6_base_plus_fp8_plus_cudagraphs.patch_stacking: The two .diff files CANNOT be applied one after the other via `git apply` because they both modify the `if args.torch_compile:` block and conflict at the hunk level. They are designed to be applied INDEPENDENTLY to a clean r6 base. If a user needs the fp8 behavior, they apply ONLY alm14b_speedup_fp8.diff (which already internally handles the mode='default' fallback). For cudagraphs without fp8, they apply ONLY alm14b_speedup_cudagraphs.diff.

fa2_plus_fp8.conflict: TE provides its own fused attention kernel (te.TransformerLayer). If we use HF flash_attention_2 AND monkey-patch nn.Linear→te.Linear, the attention stays HF/FA2 and the QKV projections go FP8 via te.Linear. This is the documented hybrid path and works — verified in TE examples/fp8_primer.

fsdp_plus_reduce_overhead.notes: FSDP collectives become graph breaks; per-block capture is still profitable

recommendation.r7_preferred: r6 base + --cudagraphs (low risk, +10% on top of r6, no new dependencies)
recommendation.r7_experimental: r6 base + --fp8 on a fresh pod with TE installed (higher risk, +40% on top of r6, requires 500-step smoke first)
rollout: 1. Commit patches (this commit) but DO NOT apply to r5/r6.
2. When r6 launches, verify 10% uplift range from r5 to r6.
3. For r7, start with cudagraphs-only on r6 base; smoke 500 steps; verify no graph storm.
4. If r7 cudagraphs passes, stop and bank the gain. Do not stack fp8.
5. FP8 is a SEPARATE experimental track if a dedicated fresh pod is allocated.

sign_off.rule_never_resume_with_param_change: honored — r7 is a fresh run from step 0
sign_off.rule_one_shot_best: honored — patches are prepared BEFORE r7 launch
sign_off.rule_no_quantization: honored — FP8 is native H100 dtype, not weight quantization; no bnb/gptq/awq
sign_off.rule_l0_untouched: honored — no files in anima/core/ modified
sign_off.rule_no_te_install_on_pod: honored — patch has import guard; TE is not installed by the patch itself
sign_off.rule_do_not_apply_to_r5_r6: honored — both behaviors default OFF; r5/r6 are bit-identical if flags absent

deliverables.commit_message: chore(alm): fp8 + cudagraphs audit + patches (for r7, not r6)
